self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n296\t        return self._zai_client\n297\t\n298\t    def _load_prompt(self, path: Path, fallback: str) -> str:\n299\t        \"\"\"Load a self-modification prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n300\t        try:\n301\t            return path.read_text()\n302\t        except Exception as e:\n303\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load prompt Args:\n329\t            instruction: Natural language instruction\n330\t\n331\t        Returns:\n332\t            The proposed diff for user approval\n333\t        \"\"\"\n334\t        # Parse the _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n359\t        \"\"\"\n360\t        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n361\t\n362\t        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n363\t        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n364\t        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n365\t        \"\"\"\n366\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, classification\n379\t                max_tokens=512,\n380\t                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n381\t            )\n382\t            data = registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n396\t            registered_set = set(registered)\n397\t            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n398\t                not name or name not in registered_set\n399\t            ):\n400\t                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n401\t                    name = \"router\"\n402\t                elif registered:\n403\t                    name = registered[0]\n404\t                else:\n405\t                    name = \"unknown\"\n406\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n407\t\n408\t            return ModificationRequest(\n409\t                instruction=instruction,\n410\t                artifact_name=name,\n411\t                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n412\t                context={\n413\t                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n414\t                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n415\t                },\n416\t            )\n417\t        except Exception as e:\n418\t            logger.warning(\n419\t                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n425\t                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n426\t            )\n427\t\n428\t    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n437\t                if artifact:\n438\t                    return artifact.content\n439\t\n440\t        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n441\t\n442\t    async def _generate_update(\n443\t        self,\n444\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n445\t        current_content: str\n446\t    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n447\t        \"\"\"\n448\t        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n449\t\n450\t        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n451\t        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n452\t        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n453\t        \"\"\"\n454\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n455\t            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n456\t        )\n457\t        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n458\t        user_message = Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n462\t        )\n463\t\n464\t        try:\n465\t            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n466\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n467\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n468\t                user_message=user_message,\n469\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n470\t                max_tokens=4096,\n471\t                temperature=0.2,\n472\t            )\n473\t            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n474\t\n475\t logger.warning(\n479\t                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n480\t                )\n481\t                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n482\t            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n483\t        except Exception as e:\n484\t            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n485\t            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n486\t\n487\t    def _estimate_confidence(\n488\t        self,\n489\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n490\t        change_summary: str\n491\t    ) -> float:\n492\t        \"\"\"\n493\t        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n494\t\n495\t        Higher confidence for:\n496\t        - Clear, specific instructions\n497\t        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n498\t        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n499\t        \"\"\"\n500\t        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n501\t\n502\t        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n503\t\n504\t        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n505\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n506\t            confidence += 0.2\n507\t\n508\t        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n509\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n510\t            confidence -= 0.2\n511\t\n512\t        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n513\t        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n514\t            confidence -= 0.1\n515\t\n516\t        # Clamp to valid range\n517\t        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n518\t\n519\t    def apply_diff(self, diff: Generate a standardized commit message for self-modification writes.\n\n    Creates a commit message with the format:\n    'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n    where <commit-short-sha> is the short SHA of the current HEAD (the commit\n    we are building on top of).\n\n    Args:\n        file_path: Path to the file being modified (relative or absolute)\n        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n\n    Returns:\n        Commit message string with path and optional previous commit SHA\n    \"\"\"\n    if cwd is None:\n        cwd = \"\"\"Build the list of registered artifacts for the parser prompt.\"\"\"\n        artifacts: List[Dict[str, str]] = []\n        for name, path_str in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().items():\n            suffix = Path(path_str).suffix.lower()\n            type_str = \"config\" if suffix in (\".yaml\", \".yml\") else \"prompt\"\n            artifacts.append({\"name\": name, \"type\": type_str})\n        return artifacts\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _strip_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Strip ```json ... ``` markdown fences from a GLM response.\"\"\"\n        raw = raw.strip()\n Returns:\n            The proposed diff for user approval\n        \"\"\"\n        # Parse the instruction to identify target (LLM call)\n        request = await self._parse_instruction(instruction)\n\n        # Get current content\n        current_content = self._get_artifact_content(request)\n\n        # Generate update (LLM call)\n        updated_content, \"unknown\",\n            artifact_type=request.artifact_type or ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=current_content,\n            after=updated_content,\n _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n\n        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, _PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK)\n        user_message = (\n            \"## Registered Artifacts\\n\"\n            + json.dumps(self._available_artifacts(), indent=2)\n            + f\"\\n\\n## User Instruction\\n{instruction}\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # cheap, fast classification\n                max_tokens=512,\n                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            type_str = data.get(\"artifact_type\", \"prompt\")\n            name = data.get(\"artifact_name\")\n\n            try:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType(type_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            # Validate the name is actually registered; fall back to a known\n            # artifact (preferring the router prompt) so we never target a\n            # non-existent artifact.\n            registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n            registered_set = set(registered)\n            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n                not name or name not in registered_set\n            ):\n                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n                    name = \"router\"\n                elif registered:\n                    name = registered[0]\n                else:\n                    name = \"unknown\"\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=name,\n                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n                context={\n                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                },\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: {e}\"\n            )\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=\"router\",\n                artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n            )\n\n    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: ModificationRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current content of the target artifact.\"\"\"\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                return self.reload_mgr.get_prompt(request.artifact_name)\n        elif request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                # For configs, return YAML as string for diff\n                artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n                if artifact:\n                    return artifact.content\n\n        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n\n    async def _generate_update(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        current_content: str\n    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n\n        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n        )\n        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n        user_message = (\n            f\"## Instruction\\n{request.instruction}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Artifact Type\\n{artifact_type}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Current Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.2,\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n                )\n                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n\n    def _estimate_confidence(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        change_summary: str\n    ) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n\n        Higher confidence for:\n        - Clear, specific instructions\n        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n        \"\"\"\n        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n\n        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n\n        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n            confidence += 0.2\n\n        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n            confidence -= 0.2\n\n        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            confidence -= 0.1\n\n        # Clamp to valid range\n        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n\n    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n\n        Args:\n            diff: The diff to apply\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successful, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Check freeze protection before writing\n            ensure_unfrozen()\n\n            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n                return self._write_prompt(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n                return self._write_config(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n                return self._write_component(diff)\n            return False\n        except RuntimeError as e:\n            # Clear error for freeze protection\n            print(f\"Cannot apply diff: {e}\")\n            return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Failed to apply diff: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n\n        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n\n        Args:\n            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get the repo root directory\n            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n\n            # Get relative path from repo root\n            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n\n            # Verify the file exists before trying to stage it\n            if not artifact_path.exists():\n                logger.error(f\"Cannot commit artifact write: file does not exist at {artifact_path}\")\n                return\n\n            # Stage the file for commit\n            # This works for new files, modified files, and files that were deleted then recreated\n            add_result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            if add_result.returncode != 0:\n                logger.error(f\"git add failed: stderr={add_result.stderr}, stdout={add_result.stdout}, returncode={add_result.returncode}\")\n                return\n\n            # Generate standardized commit message with previous commit SHA\n            commit_msg = generate_self_mod_commit_message(rel_path, cwd=repo_root)\n\n            # Create the commit (file is now staged)\n            # Only commit staged changes, ignoring other unstaged changes\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            # Only log if commit was successful\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n                    cwd=repo_root,\n                    capture_output=True,\n                    text=True,\n                    check=True,\n                    timeout=10\n                )\n\n                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n\n    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload to pick up changes\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated config file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the config write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_component(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated component to library.\"\"\"\n        # For components, we need to identify the component\n        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n        if not diff.artifact_name.startswith(\"comp-\"):\n            return False\n\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not component:\n            return False\n\n        self.component_library.update_component(\n            component.id,\n            diff.after,\n            diff.change_summary\n        )\n        return True\n\n    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n\n    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n\n        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n        For components: use component version history\n        \"\"\"\n        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n\n        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n        import subprocess\n        try:\n            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n            if not artifact:\n                return False\n\n            # Get previous version from git\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n            )\n\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n                    f.write(result.stdout)\n                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n\n        return False\n\n    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n        if not component or component.version <= 1:\n            return False\n\n        target_version = component.version - 1\n        self.component_library.rollback_component(component_id, target_version)\n        return True\n\n    def list_pending_diffs(self) -> List[ArtifactDiff]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending diffs awaiting approval.\"\"\"\n        return self._pending_diffs.copy()\n\n    def clear_pending_diffs(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all pending diffs.\"\"\"\n        self._pending_diffs.clear()\n\n\n# Singleton instance\n_agent: Optional[SelfModificationAgent] = None\n\n\ndef get_self_modification_agent() -> SelfModificationAgent:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the self-modification agent singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _agent\n    if _agent is None:\n        _agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n    return _agent\n","numLines":743,"startLine":1,"totalLines":743}}}

1\n                if msg_idx < len(cmd):\n                    captured_commit_msg.append(cmd[msg_idx])\n\n            if 'git' in cmd and 'commit' in cmd:\n                from subprocess import test_restore_artifacts_reverts_self_mod_commits(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"restore-artifacts reverts git commits created by self-modification writes.\"\"\"\n        import subprocess\n\n        # Initialize a git repo\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'init'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'config', 'user.email', 'test@example.com'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'config', 'user.name', 'Test User'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n\n        # Create a test file and commit it (baseline)\n        test_file = tmp_path / \"test_prompt.md\"\n        test_file.write_text(\"# Original content\\n\")\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'add', 'test_prompt.md'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'commit', '-m', 'baseline commit'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n\n        # Get the baseline commit SHA\n        baseline_result = subprocess.run(['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)\n        baseline_sha = baseline_result.stdout.strip()\n\n        # Simulate a self-modification write (update the file)\n        test_file.write_text(\"# Updated content by self-mod\\n\")\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'add', 'test_prompt.md'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'commit', '-m', f'auto: self-mod write to test_prompt.md [{baseline_sha}]'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n\n        # Verify the file has the updated content\n        assert test_file.read_text() == \"# Updated content by self-mod\\n\"\n\n        # Now revert the self-mod commit using git revert\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'revert', '--no-commit', 'HEAD'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n        subprocess.run(['git', 'commit', '-m', 'adc restore-artifacts: revert 1 self-mod commit(s)'], cwd=tmp_path, capture_output=True, check=True)\n\n        # Verify the file is back to the original content\n        assert test_file.read_text() == \"# Original content\\n\"\n\n    def Generate updated content based on the proposal\n447\t            # For now, we'll surface the proposal as-is and let the user approve\n448\t            # The actual content generation would be done by the self-modification agent\n449\t            # if this were a direct instruction. For background analysis, we create\n450\t            # a diff that the agent can apply.\n451\t            updated_content = current_content  # Placeholder - would be LLM-generated\n452\t\n453\t            # Create a simple diff - in production, this would be LLM-generated\n454\t            # For now, we create a minimal diff that can be reviewed\n455\t            diff = ArtifactDiff(\n456\t                artifact_name=proposal.artifact_name,\n457\t                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n458\t                before=current_content,\n459\t                after=updated_content,  # This would _auto_apply_proposal(self, proposal: AnalysisProposal) -> bool:\n470\t        \"\"\"\n471\t        Auto-apply a high-confidence proposal through self-modification write path.\n472\t\n473\t        This ensures:\n474\t        - Freeze protection is respected (via SelfModificationAgent.ensure_unfrozen)\n475\t        - Git commits are created (via SelfModificationAgent._commit_artifact_write)\n476\t        - Write scope is limited to prompts/ and config/\n477\t\n478\t        Args:\n479\t            proposal: The proposal to auto-apply\n480\t\n481\t        Returns:\n482\t            True if applied successfully, False otherwise\n483\t        \"\"\"\n484\t        try:\n485\t            # Check freeze status before attempting\n486\t            freeze_status = check_frozen()\n487\t            if freeze_status.is_frozen:\n488\t                logger.info(\n489\t                    f\"Auto-apply blocked by freeze: {proposal.proposal_id} \"\n490\t                    f\"({freeze_status.reason})\"\n491\t                )\n492\t                return False\n493\t\n494\t            # Check confidence threshold\n495\t            if proposal.confidence < self.AUTO_APPLY_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:\n496\t                logger.info(\n497\t                    f\"Proposal {proposal.proposal_id} below auto-apply threshold \"\n498\t                    f\"({proposal.confidence} < {self.AUTO_APPLY_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD})\"\n499\t                )\n500\t                return False\n501\t\n502\t            # Convert proposal to diff\n503\t            diff = self._proposal_to_diff(proposal)\n504\t            if diff is None:\n505\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to convert proposal {proposal.proposal_id} to diff\")\n506\t                return False\n507\t\n508\t            # Apply through self-modification agent (this handles freeze check and git commit)\n509\t            success = self.self_mod_agent.apply_diff(diff)\n510\t\n511\t            if success:\n512\t                logger.info(\n513\t                    f\"Auto-applied proposal {proposal.proposal_id}: \"\n514\t                    f\"{proposal.change_summary}\"\n515\t                )\n516\t            else:\n517\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to auto-apply proposal {proposal.proposal_id}\")\n518\t\n519\t            return success\n520\t\n521\t        except Exception as e:\n522\t            logger.error(f\"Error auto-applying proposal {proposal.proposal_id}: {e}\")\n523\t            return False\n524\t\n525\t    async def run(self) -> None:\n526\t        \"\"\"Main loop: periodically analyze signals and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n527\t        logger.info(f\"Starting background analysis processor (interval: {self.check_interval}s)\")\n528\t\n529\t        while self.running:\n530\t            try:\n531\t                # Analyze signals\n532\t                proposals = await                logger.error(f\"Error in background analysis loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n566\t                await asyncio.sleep(self.check_interval)\n567\t\n568\t        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor stopped\")\n569\t\n570\t    async def start(self) -> None:\n571\t        \"\"\"Start the background analysis processor.\"\"\"\n572\t        if self.task is not None and not self.task.done():\n573\t            logger.warning(\"Background analysis processor already running\")\n574\t            return\n575\t\n576\t        self.running = True\n577\t        self.task = asyncio.create_task(self.run())\n578\t        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor started\")\n579\t\n580\t    async def stop(self) -> None:\n581\t        \"\"\"Stop the background analysis processor.\"\"\"\n582\t        self.running = False\n583\t        if self.task:\n584\t            self.task.cancel()\n585\t            try:\n586\t                await self.task\n587\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n588\t                pass\n589\t        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor stopped\")\n590\t\n591\t\n592\t# Global background analysis processor instance\n593\t_background_processor: Optional[BackgroundAnalysisProcessor] = None\n594\t\n595\t\n596\tdef get_background_processor(\n597\t    signal_threshold: int = 10,\n598\t    check_interval: int analysis processor for implicit feedback signals.\n\nReads feedback signals from the session store and proposes artifact updates\nas canvas cards for the self-modification agent to process.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom ..agents.self_modification import SelfModificationAgent, get_self_modification_agent, ArtifactDiff, ArtifactType\nfrom ..sse.broadcaster import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent\nfrom ..freeze import check_frozen\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass AnalysisTrigger(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Triggers for background analysis.\"\"\"\n    SIGNAL_COUNT_THRESHOLD = \"signal_count_threshold\"  # Process after N signals\n    TIME_INTERVAL = \"time_interval\"  # Process every N seconds\n    HIGH_URGENCY_SIGNAL = \"high_urgency_signal\"  # Process immediately for critical signals\n    MANUAL = \"manual\"  # Triggered by user command\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AnalysisProposal:\n    \"\"\"A proposal for artifact update based on signal analysis.\"\"\"\n    proposal_id: str\n    signal_type: str\n    artifact_type: str  # 'prompt', 'config', 'component'\n    artifact_name: str\n    change_summary: str\n    confidence: float\n    signals_consulted: int\n    generated_at: int\n    session_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)\n    data: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_canvas_card(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to canvas card format for surfacing to user.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"id\": self.proposal_id,\n            \"type\": \"artifact_update_proposal\",\n            \"title\": f\"Update {self.artifact_type}: {self.artifact_name}\",\n            \"summary\": self.change_summary,\n            \"urgency\": \"normal\" if self.confidence < 0.8 else \"high\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"artifact_type\": self.artifact_type,\n                \"artifact_name\": self.artifact_name,\n                \"change_summary\": self.change_summary,\n                \"confidence\": self.confidence,\n                \"signals_consulted\": self.signals_consulted,\n            },\n            \"actions\": [\n                {\"id\": \"approve\", \"label\": \"Approve\", \"type\": \"primary\"},\n                {\"id\": \"reject\", \"label\": \"Reject\", \"type\": \"secondary\"},\n                {\"id\": \"defer\", \"label\": \"Defer\", \"type\": \"tertiary\"},\n            ],\n            \"created_at\": self.generated_at,\n        }\n\n\nclass BackgroundAnalysisProcessor:\n    \"\"\"\n    Processes implicit feedback signals and proposes artifact updates.\n\n    Runs as a background task that:\n    1. Reads unprocessed feedback signals from the session store\n    2. Analyzes patterns in the signals\n    3. Proposes artifact updates via self-modification agent\n    4. Surfaces proposals as canvas cards\n    5. Auto-applies high-confidence proposals through self-modification write path\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Confidence threshold for auto-apply (0.85 = 85% confidence required)\n    AUTO_APPLY_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.85\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        signal_threshold: int = 10,\n        check_interval: int = 60,\n        auto_apply_enabled: bool = True,\n    ):\n        self.signal_threshold = signal_threshold\n        self.check_interval = check_interval\n        self.auto_apply_enabled = auto_apply_enabled\n        self.running = False\n        self.task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n        self.store = get_store()\n        self.self_mod_agent = get_self_modification_agent()\n\n    async def analyze_signals(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze unprocessed feedback signals and generate proposals.\n\n        Args:\n            limit: Maximum number of signals to process in one batch\n\n        Returns:\n            List of proposals for artifact updates\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get unprocessed signals\n        signals = await self.store.get_unprocessed_signals(limit=limit)\n\n        if not signals:\n            logger.debug(\"No unprocessed signals to analyze\")\n            return []\n\n        logger.info(f\"Analyzing {len(signals)} feedback signals\")\n\n        # Group signals by type\n        signals_by_type = self._group_by_type(signals)\n\n        # Generate proposals for each signal type\n        proposals = []\n\n        for signal_type, type_signals in signals_by_type.items():\n            type_proposals = await self._analyze_signal_type(signal_type, type_signals)\n            proposals.extend(type_proposals)\n\n        # Mark processed signals\n        signal_ids = [s[\"signal_id\"] for s in signals]\n        await self.store.mark_signals_processed(signal_ids)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Generated {len(proposals)} proposals from {len(signals)} signals\")\n\n        return proposals\n\n    def _group_by_type(self, signals: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:\n        \"\"\"Group signals by type.\"\"\"\n        grouped = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            signal_type = signal[\"signal_type\"]\n            if signal_type not in grouped:\n                grouped[signal_type] = []\n            grouped[signal_type].append(signal)\n        return grouped\n\n    async def _analyze_signal_type(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str,\n        signals: list[dict],\n    ) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"Analyze signals of a specific type and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Extract unique session_ids from these signals\n        session_ids = set(s.get(\"session_id\") for s in signals if s.get(\"session_id\"))\n\n        if signal_type == \"ack_speed\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_ack_speed(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"follow_up_pattern\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_follow_up_patterns(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"surface_switch\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_surface_switches(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"result_requery\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_requeries(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"result_skipped\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_skipped_results(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"topic_continuation\":\n            # Topic continuation signals are informational, don't generate proposals\n            pass\n        elif signal_type == \"topic_switch\":\n            # Topic switches are informational, don't generate proposals\n            pass\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_ack_speed(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze acknowledgment speed signals.\n\n        Slow acks may indicate:\n        - Poor narration quality (for audio)\n        - Unclear result formatting\n        - Wrong delivery surface\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Calculate average ack delay\n        delays = []\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n            delays.append(delay)\n\n        if not delays:\n            return proposals\n\n        avg_delay = sum(delays) / len(delays)\n\n        # If average ack delay is high, propose improvements\n        if avg_delay > 60:  # More than 1 minute average\n            # Check which surface type has the slowest acks\n            surface_delays = {}\n            for signal in signals:\n                data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n                surface = data.get(\"surface_type\", \"unknown\")\n                delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n                if surface not in surface_delays:\n                    surface_delays[surface] = []\n                surface_delays[surface].append(delay)\n\n            # Find worst surface\n            worst_surface = None\n            worst_avg = 0\n            for surface, s_delays in surface_delays.items():\n                s_avg = sum(s_delays) / len(s_delays)\n                if s_avg > worst_avg:\n                    worst_avg = s_avg\n                    worst_surface = surface\n\n            if worst_surface == \"audio\":\n                # Propose improving narration prompts\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                    artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s (target: <30s). Consider shorter, more concise summaries.\",\n                    confidence=0.7,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    session_ids=session_ids,\n                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n                ))\n            elif worst_surface == \"canvas\":\n                # Propose improving result formatting\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n                    artifact_type=\"component\",\n                    artifact_name=\"result_card\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve result card formatting: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s. Consider more prominent summaries and clearer action buttons.\",\n                    confidence=0.65,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    session_ids=session_ids,\n                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n                ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_follow_up_patterns(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze follow-up pattern signals.\n\n        Follow-up patterns indicate what was missing from previous results.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count follow-up types\n        follow_up_types = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            f_type = data.get(\"follow_up_type\", \"unknown\")\n            if f_type not in follow_up_types:\n                follow_up_types[f_type] = 0\n            follow_up_types[f_type] += 1\n\n        # Generate proposals for common follow-up types\n        for f_type, count in follow_up_types.items():\n            if count >= 3:  # Need at least 3 similar follow-ups to propose change\n                if f_type == \"why_question\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_explainatory\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Add more 'why' explanations to results. {count} users asked 'why' follow-up questions recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.8,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        session_ids=session_ids,\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n                elif f_type == \"temporal_question\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_timing\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Add timing information to results. {count} users asked 'how long/when' follow-up questions recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.75,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        session_ids=session_ids,\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n                elif f_type == \"more_detail\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_verbose\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Provide more detailed results by default. {count} users asked for more detail recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.7,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        session_ids=session_ids,\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_surface_switches(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze surface switch signals.\n\n        Audio-to-canvas switches with pending results indicate poor narration.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count audio-to-canvas switches with pending results\n        audio_to_canvas_count = 0\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            if data.get(\"reason\") == \"audio_to_canvas_with_pending\":\n                audio_to_canvas_count += 1\n\n        if audio_to_canvas_count >= 5:\n            proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=\"surface_switch\",\n                artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n                change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration quality. {audio_to_canvas_count} users switched to canvas with pending results, indicating narration was insufficient.\",\n                confidence=0.85,\n                signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                session_ids=session_ids,\n                data={\"audio_to_canvas_count\": audio_to_canvas_count},\n            ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_requeries(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze re-query signals.\n\n        Re-queries indicate inadequate results.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Group by utterance to find frequently re-queried items\n        requeries = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            utterance = data.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n            attempt_count = data.get(\"attempt_count\", 0)\n            if utterance and attempt_count > 1:\n                if utterance not in requeries:\n                    requeries[utterance] = 0\n                requeries[utterance] = max(requeries[utterance], attempt_count)\n\n        # Find queries with 3+ attempts\n        for utterance, max_attempts in requeries.items():\n            if max_attempts >= 3:\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"result_requery\",\n                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                    artifact_name=\"synthesize_completeness\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve result completeness for query type: '{utterance[:50]}...'. Users re-queried {max_attempts} times.\",\n                    confidence=0.75,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    session_ids=session_ids,\n                    data={\"utterance\": utterance, \"max_attempts\": max_attempts},\n                ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_skipped_results(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze skipped result signals.\n\n        Skipped results indicate low interest or poor delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count skipped results\n        skipped_count = len(signals)\n\n        if skipped_count >= 10:\n            proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=\"result_skipped\",\n                artifact_type=\"config\",\n                artifact_name=\"monitoring\",\n                change_summary=f\"Reduce monitoring verbosity. {skipped_count} results were skipped (not acknowledged) before surface disconnect.\",\n                confidence=0.6,\n                signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                session_ids=session_ids,\n                data={\"skipped_count\": skipped_count},\n            ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    def _proposal_to_diff(self, proposal: AnalysisProposal) -> Optional[ArtifactDiff]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convert an AnalysisProposal to an ArtifactDiff for self-modification agent.\n\n        Args:\n            proposal: The analysis proposal to convert\n\n        Returns:\n            ArtifactDiff if conversion successful, None otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Map artifact_type string to ArtifactType enum\n            artifact_type_map = {\n                \"prompt\": ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n                \"config\": ArtifactType.CONFIG,\n                \"component\": ArtifactType.COMPONENT,\n            }\n\n            artifact_type = artifact_type_map.get(proposal.artifact_type)\n            if artifact_type is None:\n                logger.warning(f\"Unknown artifact type: {proposal.artifact_type}\")\n                return None\n\n            # Get current artifact content\n            current_content = \"\"\n            if proposal.artifact_name in self.self_mod_agent.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                if artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n                    current_content = self.self_mod_agent.reload_mgr.get_prompt(proposal.artifact_name)\n                elif artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n                    artifact = self.self_mod_agent.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(proposal.artifact_name)\n                    if artifact:\n                        current_content = artifact.content\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Artifact not found: {proposal.artifact_name}\")\n                return None\n\n            # Generate updated content based on the proposal\n            # For now, we'll surface the proposal as-is and let the user approve\n            # The actual content generation would be done by the self-modification agent\n            # if this were a direct instruction. For background analysis, we create\n            # a diff that the agent can apply.\n            updated_content = current_content  # Placeholder - would be LLM-generated\n\n            # Create a simple diff - in production, this would be LLM-generated\n            # For now, we create a minimal diff that can be reviewed\n            diff = ArtifactDiff(\n                artifact_name=proposal.artifact_name,\n                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n                before=current_content,\n                after=updated_content,  # This would be LLM-generated in production\n                change_summary=proposal.change_summary,\n                confidence=proposal.confidence,\n            )\n\n            return diff\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to convert proposal to diff: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _auto_apply_proposal(self, proposal: AnalysisProposal) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Auto-apply a high-confidence proposal through self-modification write path.\n\n        This ensures:\n        - Freeze protection is respected (via SelfModificationAgent.ensure_unfrozen)\n        - Git commits are created (via SelfModificationAgent._commit_artifact_write)\n        - Write scope is limited to prompts/ and config/\n\n        Args:\n            proposal: The proposal to auto-apply\n\n        Returns:\n            True if applied successfully, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Check freeze status before attempting\n            freeze_status = check_frozen()\n            if freeze_status.is_frozen:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Auto-apply blocked by freeze: {proposal.proposal_id} \"\n                    f\"({freeze_status.reason})\"\n                )\n                return False\n\n            # Check confidence threshold\n            if proposal.confidence < self.AUTO_APPLY_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Proposal {proposal.proposal_id} below auto-apply threshold \"\n                    f\"({proposal.confidence} < {self.AUTO_APPLY_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD})\"\n                )\n                return False\n\n            # Convert proposal to diff\n            diff = self._proposal_to_diff(proposal)\n            if diff is None:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to convert proposal {proposal.proposal_id} to diff\")\n                return False\n\n            # Apply through self-modification agent (this handles freeze check and git commit)\n            success = self.self_mod_agent.apply_diff(diff)\n\n            if success:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Auto-applied proposal {proposal.proposal_id}: \"\n                    f\"{proposal.change_summary}\"\n                )\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to auto-apply proposal {proposal.proposal_id}\")\n\n            return success\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error auto-applying proposal {proposal.proposal_id}: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main loop: periodically analyze signals and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Starting background analysis processor (interval: {self.check_interval}s)\")\n\n        while self.running:\n            try:\n                # Analyze signals\n                proposals = await self.analyze_signals()\n\n                if proposals:\n                    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n                    for proposal in proposals:\n                        # Attempt auto-apply for high-confidence proposals\n                        auto_applied = False\n                        if self.auto_apply_enabled:\n                            auto_applied = await self._auto_apply_proposal(proposal)\n\n                        # If not auto-applied, surface as canvas card for manual review\n                        if not auto_applied:\n                            card = proposal.to_canvas_card()\n\n                            # Broadcast to each relevant session\n                            for session_id in proposal.session_ids:\n                                event = SSEEvent(\n                                    event_type=\"artifact_proposal\",\n                                    data=card,\n                                    target_session_id=session_id,\n                                )\n                                sent_count = await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n                                logger.info(\n                                    f\"Broadcast proposal {proposal.proposal_id} to session {session_id}: \"\n                                    f\"{proposal.change_summary} (sent to {sent_count} connections)\"\n                                )\n\n                # \"\"\"\n    Manage self-modification freeze state.\n\n    View or toggle the freeze protection that blocks self-modification writes.\n\n    Args:\n        toggle: If True, toggle freeze state (create or remove sentinel file)\n\n    Returns:\n        Exit code (0 for success, non-zero for error)\n    \"\"\"\n    from src.freeze import get_status, set_frozen\n\n    if toggle:\n        # Get current state before toggling\n        current = get_status()\n        new_state = not current[\"frozen\"]\n        set_frozen(new_state)\n\n        if new_state:\n            print(\"✓ Self-modification frozen (created data/FREEZE)\")\n            print(\"  To unfreeze, run: adc freeze --toggle\")\n        else:\n            print(\"✓ Self-modification unfrozen (removed data/FREEZE)\")\n enabled.\")\n        print(\"  To freeze: adc freeze --toggle\\n\")\n\n    return 0\n\n\ndef restore_artifacts_cmd(commits: int = 1, dry_run: bool = False) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Restore artifacts from git history by reverting self-mod commits.\n\n    Reverts the specified number of self-modification commits, automatically\n    clearing any freeze state before reverting. Self-mod commits are identified\n    by their commit message pattern: 'auto: self-mod write to ...'\n\n    Args:\n        commits: Number of self-mod commits to revert (default: 1)\n        dry_run: If True, show what would be reverted without making changes\n\n    Returns:\n        Exit code (0 for success, non-zero for error)\n    \"\"\"\n    import subprocess\n    from pathlib import Path\n\n    from src.freeze import set_frozen, check_frozen\n\n    print(\"\\n🔄 Artifact Restore\\n\")\n\n    # Check current freeze state\n    status = check_frozen()\n    was_frozen = status.is_frozen\n\n    if was_frozen:\n        print(f\"🔓 Unfreezing before restore (was: {status.reason})\")\n        set_frozen(False)\n        print(\"✓ Unfrozen\\n\")\n\n    # Get the repo root\n    repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n\n    try:\n        # Find self-mod commits\n        # Git log command to find commits matching the pattern\n        # Format: %h for short hash, %s for subject\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            ['git', 'log', '--oneline', '-n', str(commits * 2)],  # Get extra to filter\n            cwd=repo_root,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            timeout=10\n        )\n\n        if result.returncode != 0:\n            print(f\"❌ Failed to get git log: {result.stderr}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return 1\n\n        # Parse commits to find self-mod ones\n        all_commits = []\n        for line in result.stdout.strip().split('\\n'):\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split(' ', 1)\n            if len(parts) == 2:\n                short_hash, subject = parts\n                if 'auto: self-mod write to' in subject:\n                    all_commits.append((short_hash, subject))\n\n        # Limit to requested number\n        self_mod_commits = all_commits[:commits]\n\n        if not self_mod_commits:\n            print(\"ℹ️  No self-modification commits found to revert.\")\n            if was_frozen:\n                print(\"🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n                set_frozen(True)\n            return 0\n\n        print(f\"Found {len(self_mod_commits)} self-mod commit(s) to revert:\\n\")\n        for i, (short_hash, subject) in enumerate(self_mod_commits, 1):\n            print(f\"  {i}. {short_hash} - {subject}\")\n\n        if dry_run:\n            print(\"\\n🏁 Dry run complete - no changes made.\")\n            if was_frozen:\n                print(\"🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n                set_frozen(True)\n            return 0\n\n        print(\"\\n🔄 Reverting commits...\")\n\n        # Revert each commit in reverse order (oldest first)\n        for short_hash, _ in reversed(self_mod_commits):\n            print(f\"  Reverting {short_hash}...\", end=\" \")\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'revert', '--no-commit', short_hash],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,\n                timeout=30\n            )\n\n            if result.returncode != 0:\n                print(f\"❌\")\n                print(f\"    Failed: {result.stderr}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                # Abort the revert on failure\n                subprocess.run(['git', 'revert', '--abort'], cwd=repo_root, capture_output=True)\n                if was_frozen:\n                    print(\"🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n                    set_frozen(True)\n                return 1\n\n            print(\"✓\")\n\n        # Commit the revert\n        print(\"\\n💾 Committing revert...\", end=\" \")\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            ['git', 'commit', '-m', f\"adc restore-artifacts: revert {len(self_mod_commits)} self-mod commit(s)\"],\n            cwd=repo_root,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=False,\n            timeout=10\n        )\n\n        if result.returncode != 0:\n            print(f\"❌\")\n            print(f\"    Failed to commit: {result.stderr}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            if was_frozen:\n                print(\"🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n                set_frozen(True)\n            return 1\n\n        print(\"✓\")\n        print(f\"\\n✅ Successfully reverted {len(self_mod_commits)} self-mod commit(s)\")\n        print(\"   Artifacts restored to previous version.\")\n\n        # Re-freeze if it was frozen before\n        if was_frozen:\n            print(\"\\n🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n            set_frozen(True)\n            print(\"✓ Re-frozen\")\n\n        return 0\n\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        print(\"❌ Git command timed out\", file=sys.stderr)\n        if was_frozen:\n            print(\"🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n            set_frozen(True)\n        return 1\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"❌ Restore failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        if was_frozen:\n            print(\"🔓 Re-freezing (restore state)\")\n            set_frozen(True)\n        return 1\n","numLines":633,"startLine":1,"totalLines":633}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_cd45089e6b834d8ba68b0d15","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n4\t\n5\tCLI alias: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## How It Works\n10\t\n11\t1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n12\t\n13\t2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## Quick Start\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\t# Clone and install\n87\tgit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\n88\tcd aide-de-camp\n89\t\n90\tpip install -e \".[dev]\"\n91\t# or\n92\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n93\t\n94\t# Start the server\n95\tuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n96\t\n97\t# Verify it's running\n98\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n99\t```\n100\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e5db08f7-9eaa-4f3d-95a4-c205af7a85c1","uuid":"cfeefed4-6b74-43d8-9e54-52a0a246da8b","timestamp":"2026-07-24T01:16:36.906Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/README.md","content":"# aide-de-camp\n\n**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n\nCLI alias: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n\n2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":236}}}

Two escape hatches bypass the interface entirely: `ADC_SELFMOD_FREEZE=1` in the server environment, or `adc freeze` from the CLI (which falls back to touching a `data/FREEZE` sentinel file if the server is unresponsive). While frozen, every self-modification and auto-apply write is refused, and `adc restore-artifacts` git-reverts `prompts/` and `config/` to the last-known-good commit — no canvas, voice session, or router involvement required. **Break-glass caveat:** the sentinel-file fallback and the git revert are local filesystem/git operations against the server's artifact store — `adc` is a thin HTTP client (see adc CLI connectivity), so from a remote machine neither works exactly when the server is unresponsive. The break-glass procedure is: SSH into the server host first, then run `adc freeze` / `adc restore-artifacts` there.\n/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md:Proposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold — and an auto-apply is not a third artifact writer: the accepted proposal executes through the self-modification agent's write path, inheriting its write scope (`prompts/` and `config/` only), its git-commit-per-write rollback guarantee (one-instruction revert), and the out-of-band kill switch — all auto-apply is refused while the freeze is engaged.\n/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md:The artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from. The PVC artifact directory is itself a git repository: the server commits every `PATCH /artifacts/{name}` write with the same machine-generated message convention, so the \"versioning is git\" safety model — one-instruction rollback, `adc restore-artifacts` — survives the move to Stage B unchanged.\n/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md:One exception: the kill-switch fallbacks (`adc freeze`'s `data/FREEZE` sentinel, `adc restore-artifacts`' git revert) act on the server's local filesystem, not over HTTP — they only work when `adc` runs on the server host itself. The hot-path server is the one other writer to that DB, confined to mechanical render/usage state on each dispatch: `card_cache` rows and the usage-stat columns (`components.usage_count`/`last_used`, `component_usage_patterns.sample_count`/`last_matched`) — bookkeeping, never templates or definitions. Background-analysis auto-applies are not a third artifact writer: they execute through the self-modification agent's write path, inheriting its scope, freeze/kill-switch behavior, and git-commit rollback (see Background Analysis Bead).
